description: Indo-Canadian organized crime consists of predominantly Punjabi-Canadian gangs involved in drug trafficking, murder, and other criminal activities. Originating in British Columbia in the 1980s, these groups are considered the third most sophisticated organized crime hierarchy in Canada. They maintain ties to Punjab, India, and operate across Canada, the United States, and internationally.

country_links.evidence_quote: Punjabi-Canadian crime bosses use their family connections in the Punjab to bring in heroin from the "Golden Crescent" nations of Afghanistan, Pakistan and Iran
country_links.context: The organization brings in heroin from the 'Golden Crescent' nations including Afghanistan.
The organization has areas of influence in Australia.
The organization has areas of influence in Bolivia.
The organization has areas of influence in Chile.
The organization has areas of influence in Colombia.
The organization has areas of influence in Costa Rica.
The organization has areas of influence in El Salvador.
The organization has minor connections in France.
The organization has minor connections in Germany.
The organization has areas of influence in Greece.
The organization has areas of influence in Hong Kong.

country_links.evidence_quote: Often using clan-based ties and connections in their ancestral homeland, mainly in the parts of the Punjab, organized criminals from there were able to build criminal empires making use of young street gangs.
country_links.context: The organization uses clan-based ties and connections in Punjab, India, to build criminal empires and bring in heroin.

country_links.evidence_quote: Punjabi-Canadian crime bosses use their family connections in the Punjab to bring in heroin from the "Golden Crescent" nations of Afghanistan, Pakistan and Iran
country_links.context: The organization brings in heroin from the 'Golden Crescent' nations including Iran.
The organization has areas of influence in Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: setting up connections with Mexican drug cartels and using truck drivers to smuggle cocaine from Mexico into the United States and Canada
country_links.context: The organization sets up connections with Mexican drug cartels to smuggle cocaine from Mexico.
